transcript.text: Hey, Eric, how's it going?
Hey, Will, doing good.
How are you doing?
Doing good.
This meeting is being recorded.
We're the meeting today, right?
Sorry, what was that?
Megan said she wouldn't be joining us today, so it's just the two of us.
Okay.
Hello, everyone.
Hi, Troy.
How is everyone doing?
Good, and yourself?
Doing well.
Doing very well.
We have published a bunch of blogs.
I see that since we last met.
And I don't have anything beyond really just want to understand how we now.

That we're publishing these things within SEMRush or any of the other tools, how we are sort of measuring that performance or tracking it and just trying so we can understand, you know, do we have a dashboard where we can sort of look at this and see week over week improvements on the queries that we're aiming for?
Just want to be able to understand how we can, like, start to map out the next few months of content.
And then I guess additionally about just some of the competitor pages overall as we look to revamp those beyond just the blog posts.
But that's my main things today.
Yeah.
So we do have the Looker dashboard, I believe you were invited to.
And so for the blogs that we are creating and posting, that's where you can see in the cohorts view.

We overall sessions, engagement rate for the cohort specific pieces, and then once we get down to the specific URL list, that's where you can see overall URL clicks, and so a lot of these are non-growthx URLs.
Right, right.
And so this session, engagement rate, that's from Google Analytics, I take it?
Engagement rate?
Correct.
All right.
Cool.
Architect, voice, cohorts, landing pages.
I would really love to get a view of this, yeah, without the AI apps on it.
Without the AI apps.
I don't really, I just want to understand what our growth is without that, because that's going to, you know, that's not really doing much.
It's doing much for us, so.
Yep.
So, So,
It would be great.
Can we do that?
Instruct filters, landing page.
I believe we should be able to.
Yeah, it would be great of a view without AI apps.
Yep, we can do that.
Right.
And then get a real sense for kind of what traffic looks like without those.
And yeah, as we continue to publish, you know, indexing takes place, then we get the impressions, and then clicks.
So we should see this, at least in on GrowthX, or the GrowthX URLs start to take traction.

But I think once we, now that we've kind of transitioned teams to the kind of long-term team, these will be part of a weekly update that we'll likely start with just to show traction before we jump into any kind of tactical kind of here's what we need to block and tackle with, if that makes sense.
right
Totally.
Totally.
Right, because I think now that we're sort of in now, we're moving into the content, you know, we're pushing out a lot of content.
That's awesome.
I'd want us to sort of understand, you know, what we could be doing, I guess, more from a technical SEO standpoint with some of this content, you know, with linking with on across the page.
And then I think long-term goal is for us to completely revamp, you know, I know we're putting a lot of articles on our competitors.
I think eventually I want to have a whole revamped, like, comparison page with our, you know, charts and tables and everything like that.
If you look at our, our, you know, look at them today, it's pretty just text-based and it's, they're kind of copied and pasted from each other.
They're just sort of talking about our, our value adds and not necessarily comparing them with the.
The specific capabilities of each product.
So that's something I think it would be great.
How do we kind of take this?
And it's great to have these long-form blogs.
Obviously, if someone wants to, if they are looking at this specific, we want to be at that greater level of specificity.
However, I just think that we don't need to really sharpen the content, the competitive content, to kind of a central core to make this stuff work.
Because I really want to make sure that we're showing up on, like, deepgram versus this, or, you know, so that we're not getting out-performed there.
But I think that, yeah, overall, the content we're putting out is very much going to help us.
Because it's, I think it's filling some of the content gaps we have in some of the search queries.
But, yeah, I think I want us to be able to have, that's where I want to be able to take these and turn them into that page.
Yep.
All right.
And then I know, Hassan, you just pinged about the.
The speech-to-text language pages wireframe.
Yeah, Troy, I don't know if you've been briefed on this, but basically we're trying to do language.
We support 35 plus languages for speech-to-text and then text-to-speech, pretty soon we're going to be supporting like around 10.

And so having language pages that capture SEO terms around those specific like Spanish speech-to-text or French speech-to-text and stuff like that, that's like low-hanging fruit, pretty low competitive, no competitiveness on those terms.
So we just had, we're working on a wireframe for speech-to-text, so the Spanish one first.
I did want to like share that with you to get your kind of review and eyes on it to make sure like tags, headers, and stuff all kind of just make sense or optimized.
No, yeah, that's good to hear.
Oh.
But there's still some optimizations that are needed on that page, Erik, because there are some elements that are the same across each page.
So it's, you know, we can just sort of, like, replicate it if we do other language ranges.
Some are, like, a little bit more dynamic because not every language supports every feature.
So we have some panels that are a little bit more dynamic, but they're all kind of, like, charted out in a spreadsheet.
And I believe Patrizia is going to include a loom on, or she's going to record a loom on basically how to configure the pages if certain features change.
Okay.
Awesome.
Wonderful.

Yeah, I think as soon as you guys do the internal review and could share both the wireframe and that Excel sheet, then I could take it to our engineering team and get their estimate on kind of when we can fit this into our next.

Cycle, they've been asking about it this week and last week, so I told them it was coming, but, so I think we're just, yeah, we're getting close, we're getting close, so yeah, Troy, I'll share it out with you, and there's just some slight tweaks that are probably needed from there, cool, is the Airtable updated, in terms of what's reviewed and what's published, what's being reviewed, Will, do you know?
I don't think so, looking at it now, I'm looking at the column for published, I'm not seeing any, any of the blog posts you've published, so that's.
those are all, like, everything that was under review is published.
Okay, good.
Just want to make sure, so I know what.
Perfect.
Okay, so, cool.
So, that's it.
Like, what is our current, do we have a backlog, or are we, sorry, I'm just sort of getting caught up again.
We have a backlog, but not from GrowthX.
Everything from GrowthX is published.
Okay, gotcha.
Yeah.
Okay.
The backlog's in the Notion, the content calendar.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, you can see what that looks like over there.
Okay.
And then I think from the GrowthX side, Will, we were looking at some text-to-speech, was it, Erner, speech-to-text?
Yeah,-to-speech.
Yeah, text-to-speech, yeah.
So I'm writing five new pieces on text-to-speech, which I have ready for Jose to review by the end of this week.
And Hasan, if you can review a couple of more, like 12 more, that you want me to focus on.
Or if you want, I can just go and pick about 12 to 20, and then send the list to you, and then you can let me know if you'd like me to go ahead, whichever.
What do you think is easier?
That's good.
I'll get 12 more picked out.
There is, yeah, there's probably more, there's some probably TTS topics that are, we should start thinking of covering, not anytime soon, but maybe Q1.
Um, so, uh, I can also add topics, uh, as well, or, like, how should we, um, share more topics to cover?

Yeah, you can, uh, share the topics with me, and then I can do, uh, keyword research on them, and then I can send them back to you, and once you've given us the go-ahead, I can just add them to the, the Airtable list.
Okay, that's fine.
So, for the time being, though, you just need 12 more from the existing backlog?
Exactly.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Okay.
Uh.
All right, I think that's, yeah, those are the majority of the updates for today.

There's so many, right, you know, and I think what I want to start thinking about, maybe even with, like, Justin, like, can we, you know, you know, is there a place for some of these, even within, like, our retargeting campaigns on, like, LinkedIn or, you know, some of the posts, or could they be, you know, relevant for stuff like that?
I'm not sure, like, just, you know, in terms of just use cases and listicles, things like that.
Like, I love those 10 use cases, voice agents, or, you know, that type of thing.
But I think it's great that we have one of these blog sites.

I want to figure out how do we get more of this content out into the world, whether it's syndicating it on, you know, content marketing, whether it's through our own emails, right, or how do we put it, you know, as part of our own developer marketing campaign.
So that's, you know.
Not necessarily GrowthX's response, but for the team on the deepgram side is really where do we think we can put this content?
How do we make this really work for us?

And like within email, again, email campaigns, or if we have sponsorships, editorial sponsorships on newsletter, stuff like that, that's super cheap that we could get some of the stuff if it's very related to some of the primary platforms, stuff like that could be very helpful for us.
So I love that, you we're publishing, it goes on our blog, and hopefully for SEO, but I think we can get a lot more value out of the really great content that's being produced here.
So let's think about that, about how we want to, you know, get this to our community and get it to work for us.
Because that's what's going to be part of the SEO too, right?
We have to get it linked out, we have to have people, you know, sharing it and having links and stuff like that.
So I think that'll be the next step for us is, you know, how do we want to, you know, think about our audiences, think about.
Our CRM and how we can, you know, really ensure that this stuff is being used in sales processes or wherever necessary.

So, yeah, that's just, I think, you know, Praveen and I want to be sure that this doesn't just become sort of, oh, we, you know, we're just building up a lot of content and it's going on to this blog and whatever it does is great.
think there's just been a lot of opportunities for us to put this into a lot of the other stuff that we're doing.
So, yeah, so I just want us to be really, like, how can we take all this is here and, you know, whether updating on other parts of the website, stuff like that.
really want us to, or ones that do well, Jose, like, where do we want to expand?
Where do we want to sort of continue to build more content, whether it's videos, any sort of stuff, you know, that's what I want to be tracking as well.
So, yeah.
I mean, it's just, it's a lot of, it's really good stuff.
I just want to be, I want us to be sure that we're, we're making it all work together because I think there's.
Our SEO presence is probably declining because we've not been putting out this type of content.
That's it.
I'll stop talking.
Sorry, I have kind of a cold, so I took like four pseudofeds this morning, so I'm a little, you know, messed up.
But apologies.
No, I'm just thrilled that we have, like, we're able to get this stuff going so quickly.
So I just want us to all be thinking about where it can, how we get it to more people.
Absolutely.
And then I think from a more strategic play, like, Hasan, how you mentioned the text-to-speech kind of Q1.
Any other kind of focus areas you guys are wanting to lean into as we turn into the new year?
Any other additional kind of clusters or topics that we might want to start to research and get prepped?
I think.
other Any Any

guys have adequate coverage for, like, voice agent stuff and speech-to-text, I think text-to-speech is probably the one where there's going to be more gaps that we have currently, and Q1, where we'll want to have an increased emphasis on it.
Gotcha.
Anything specific driving that, like product updates?
Yeah, we're going to have a new Texas-each model.

It's going to be a big quarter for us for text-to-speech, and generally, like, our go-to-market is going to, we're trying to increase our share of revenue that comes from text-to-speech in 2026, so that's driving it.
You'll want to take a look at, specifically, like, Eleven Labs there, because they're the sort of big elephant in the room and behemoth on Texas-beech, so they rank for all.
You can kind of just see what their strategy is because they do really well on SEO, all things text-to-speech.
I do feel like they have a lot of programmatic efforts.
Like they have not just language support, but then they have different types of voices.
The way they structure their website and do all kinds of stuff, you might want to just use those as inspiration.
Yeah, a lot of industry and vertical-specific pages as well.
And integrations, I'm just looking at it right now.

But yeah, I think we will definitely take a look at 11 Labs and just start to do the kind of blog analysis of what they're ranking for and probably outside of the blog, just what pages for them were driving traffic, and then which keywords can we kind of focus on and try to steal some share of voice from them.
Yeah, sounds good.
Yeah, the only caveat with them is...

They are more focused on a consumer and a creator audience, so that's one thing where we can, like, borrow some strategies from them we should, but, you know, we don't want to just, like, completely copy what they're doing if it's consumer-oriented.
Yeah, definitely heard.
Wonderful.
Anything else from the deepgram side?
Any questions you guys have or things we can prepare?
I was just going ask, on the language stuff, I was just going to add the content calendar.
Have we mapped that out yet, or is there work to be done in terms of, sort of, ideation for stuff on languages?
Yeah, that's a good point.
Maybe there is, I think, I thought I said there was some, a blog topic around multilingual strategies or something like that, but there's probably more blog content we could be doing around.
Right, mean, it's just, if we want to think about, like, some of the top languages, or, like,
What was it, multilingual?
Yeah, just, like, how do you, languages and stuff like that.
Right.
Yeah, let's just, if we're, if that's on, that could be a good round of next things to.
Yeah, yeah, for sure, for sure.
And it goes well with text-to-speech, too, right, in terms of.
Yeah, yeah.
Okay, that's it, that's it.
I think we're all set.
All right.
Yeah, we published so many blogs in one, it was pretty wild.
I was like, um, but yeah, and do we do, do we have, I'm sorry, another internal question.
Do we have, like, a newsletter?
We have a newsletter, right?
We do.
We have two.
We have, uh, uh, we have, like, our, basically, what we call Deepgram Digest, which is more product, like, Deepgram-oriented product news.
Right.
any, like, product updates that goes out.
And then we have, like, AI Minds, which Jose owns AI Minds.
That's more general, like, more thought leadership industry news, less, like, Deepgram-focused.
Yeah.
Go ahead, go ahead.
Yeah.
And Minds.
It focuses mainly on voice AI news.
So we publish our own blogs.
We advertise for those on there.
We also advertise our podcasts.
So the Scott AI Show podcast, as well as the AI Minds podcast with Demetrius.
And then the rest of it is like fun research and like other big news in the voice AI world.
often do we send that out?
Every other week.
Every other week.
Okay.
Yeah, they're staggered.
So something goes out every week, whether it's like our product newsletter or like AI Minds.
This week.

Yeah, I've been including.
Yeah, I've been doing the product newsletter.

So I've been including, I've been putting it in there.
Okay.
Yeah, I awesome.
Cool.

So

It's hyper-relevant, right, sort of thing.
So no answers at the moment.

just, again, want to be sure that, you know, so much effort is being put into producing these that, you know, like we do have, you know, if it's a, you know, language thing or if it's a regional thing or things like that, you know, or, you know, for our text-to-speech people who sign up for that, you know, we can start to segment this a bit, I think.
I agree, yeah.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review content performance and plan future SEO strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=48.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Performance Tracking:** The Looker dashboard will be updated to exclude low-value "AI apps" traffic, providing a clearer view of new content's performance.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)
  - [**Strategic Revamp:** The immediate focus is a major overhaul of competitor comparison pages, replacing generic text with data-rich tables to better capture "vs." search traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=263.0)
  - [**Q1 Content Push:** A major Text-to-Speech (TTS) content initiative is planned for Q1 2026 to support a new model launch and increase TTS revenue share.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=874.0)
  - [**Content Amplification:** Published content will be actively promoted beyond the blog via newsletters, targeted email campaigns, and social media to maximize its value and SEO impact.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=669.0)

## Topics

### Content Performance & Tracking

  - [**Problem:** The Looker dashboard's performance view is skewed by low-value traffic from "AI apps" pages, obscuring the impact of new content.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)
  - [**Solution:** Erik will create a new dashboard view that filters out this traffic, enabling accurate measurement of new blog performance.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)
  - [**Goal:** Use this data to inform technical SEO optimizations (e.g., internal linking) and guide the long-term content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=244.0)

### Strategic Content Revamps

  - [**Competitor Pages:** A full revamp is planned to replace generic text with data-rich tables and charts, directly comparing Deepgram's capabilities against competitors.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=263.0)
      - [**Rationale:** To capture high-intent "Deepgram vs. \[Competitor\]" search queries and outperform current text-heavy pages.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=317.0)
  - [**Language Pages:** New pages for 35+ STT and \~10 TTS languages are in development.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=366.0)
      - [**Rationale:** To capture low-competition, high-value terms (e.g., "Spanish speech-to-text").](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=366.0)
      - [**Status:** A wireframe for the Spanish STT page is ready for Troy's SEO review. The pages are designed for scalability, using a mix of static and dynamic elements managed via a spreadsheet and Loom tutorial.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=357.0)

### Q1 2026 Content Focus: Text-to-Speech (TTS)

  - [**Driver:** A new TTS model launch and a strategic goal to increase TTS revenue share in 2026.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=874.0)
  - [**Benchmark:** Eleven Labs, a market leader, will be analyzed for SEO strategy, with the caveat that Deepgram's focus is B2B, not consumer/creator.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=948.0)
  - [**Status:**](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=564.0)
      - [Will is writing 5 new TTS pieces for Jose's review this week.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=564.0)
      - [Hasan will provide 12 more TTS topics for Will to research.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)

### Content Amplification Strategy

  - [**Goal:** Maximize ROI on content by promoting it beyond the blog.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=669.0)
  - [**Current Channels:**](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=1114.0)
      - [**Deepgram Digest:** Product news newsletter; Hasan includes new blogs.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=1114.0)
      - [**AI Minds:** Bi-weekly thought leadership newsletter; Jose owns.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=1128.0)
  - [**New Opportunities:**](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=669.0)
      - [**Targeted Email Campaigns:** Send hyper-relevant content to specific user segments (e.g., TTS users, language-specific audiences).](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=1194.0)
      - [**Social Media:** Use content for LinkedIn retargeting and organic posts.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=669.0)
      - [**Syndication:** Explore editorial sponsorships in relevant newsletters.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=731.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Erik:**](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=1270.0)
      - [Create a Looker dashboard view that excludes "AI apps" traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)
      - [Get an engineering estimate for the language pages once wireframes are received.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=463.0)
  - [**Hasan:**](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=357.0)
      - [Share the Spanish STT page wireframe and configuration spreadsheet with Troy for SEO review.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=357.0)
      - [Provide Will with 12 additional TTS topics for research.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)
  - [**Will:**](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=564.0)
      - [Deliver 5 new TTS pieces to Jose for review by EOW.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=564.0)
      - [Conduct keyword research on the 12 new topics from Hasan.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=636.0)
  - [**Troy:**](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=357.0)
      - [Review the Spanish STT page wireframe for SEO optimization.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=357.0)
      - [Lead the internal discussion on a comprehensive content amplification strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/C-wPE5pkWaQSsa1xEq6HLPnz8fD7UxE-?tab=summary&timestamp=669.0)

action_items.description: Create Looker view excluding AI apps for Troy
Start weekly performance update for Troy
Share Spanish STT language-page wireframe + Excel w/ Troy & Erik; Erik routes to eng for estimate
Send 12 TTS topics to Will; Will does KR, adds to Airtable
Analyze ElevenLabs TTS SEO; share findings w/ Hasan & Troy
Add multilingual/language blog topics to content calendar
